nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 九州 (日本)
nodes.summary: 九州 (日本)是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联6种鸟类，包括黑脸琵鹭、銅長尾雉、长嘴剑鸻等6种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 本州
nodes.summary: 本州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联9种鸟类，包括銅長尾雉、普通秧鸡、长嘴剑鸻等9种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 鸡形目
nodes.summary: 鸡形目（Galliformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联298种鸟类，如眼斑冢雉、Red-billed brushturkey、Black-billed brushturkey等298种鸟类。

nodes.name: 猎捕压力
nodes.summary: 直接猎捕或误捕造成额外死亡。

nodes.name: 銅長尾雉
nodes.summary: 銅長尾雉（Copper pheasant，学名 Syrmaticus soemmerringii），分类上属于鸡形目，并归入雉科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，主要分布于本州、九州 (日本)、四國，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、猎捕压力。

nodes.name: 雉科
nodes.summary: 雉科（Phasianidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联179种鸟类，如Udzungwa forest partridge、銹紅林鷓鴣、冠鷓鴣等179种鸟类。

nodes.name: 四國
nodes.summary: 四國是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联3种鸟类，包括銅長尾雉、长嘴剑鸻、日本歌鸲，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The copper pheasant is distributed in and endemic to the hill and mountain forests of the Honshū, Kyūshū, and Shikoku islands at elevations of up to . It typically confines itself to deciduous forest floors with extensive vegetation

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The copper pheasant is distributed in and endemic to the hill and mountain forests of the Honshū, Kyūshū, and Shikoku islands at elevations of up to . It typically confines itself to deciduous forest floors with extensive vegetation

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Its population has been in consistent decline since the 1970s due to factors including habitat destruction and predation, but the most widely cited cause is overhunting

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: ijimae being listed as "Near Threatened" on the Japanese Red List. In 1995, an IUCN report called for the continued monitoring of the species, citing over-hunting and a lack of knowledge about the species' behaviour as a reason to study and protect them. Threats to the species include non-native predation, overhunting, and habitat destruction. Feral dogs and cats disturb nesting sites, further negatively impacting the species. However, overhunting is widely considered as the greatest threat to the species. Throughout the twentieth century half a million to 800,000 copper pheasants were killed by hunters every year
